name: Bharat PetroResources Limited
oneLine: Canonical pending verification with BPCL parent; assumed canonical bprlindia.com had no DNS
findings: bprlindia.com (initial assumed canonical) returned no DNS A record
bharatpetroresources.com (likely canonical) — Cloudflare front, LiteSpeed origin, scheduled-maintenance mode
bharatpetroresources.in also live (ASP.NET stack) — needs verification: legitimate alternate or impersonation

urgentActions.what: Confirm canonical domain via BPCL parent or MoPNG annual report reference

fingerprints.stack: Cloudflare front + LiteSpeed origin with WordPress
eolFlags: Google TLS WE1 expires 2026-06-11 (~44 days remaining — URGENT renewal needed)
Separate domain — canonical not yet confirmed by BPCL parent

attackPaths.name: WordPress arbitrary file upload → RCE (CVE-2024-31210 if vulnerable)
attackPaths.entry: If WordPress 6.0–6.4.2, plugin upload allows path-traversal file write. Authenticated user with upload capability.
attackPaths.pivot: Upload .zip plugin; on FTP-creds prompt, file persists in Media Library; PHP shell uploaded.
attackPaths.objective: RCE on WordPress; complete site compromise.

attackPaths.name: Cloudflare bypass → origin server attack
attackPaths.entry: Identify origin IP via DNS leaks or CT issuance trail; bypass Cloudflare WAF.
attackPaths.pivot: Port-scan origin; exploit unpatched LiteSpeed or WordPress directly.
attackPaths.objective: Direct DB and filesystem access without WAF interference.

attackPaths.name: Canonical-confusion attack across .com / .in
attackPaths.entry: Two stacks (.com WordPress, .in ASP.NET) — attacker exploits ambiguity.
attackPaths.pivot: Compromise weaker stack; phish .com users via .in 'official' content.
attackPaths.objective: Cross-domain credential harvesting; supply-chain attack on partners.

attackPaths.name: TLS expiry MITM window (44 days)
attackPaths.entry: Cert renewal not started; expiry approaches.
attackPaths.pivot: During emergency recertification, attacker presents rogue cert.
attackPaths.objective: MITM during emergency renewal; service disruption.

paths.preAi: WordPress RCE → site control + customer data + malware distribution to visitors
paths.mythos: PSU-branded site compromise; malware injected on partner portals; supply-chain vector (vendor docs trojaned with SideCopy RAT, sector-documented)

cisoPatchList.title: URGENT: Renew TLS cert before 2026-06-11 (44 days)
cisoPatchList.fix: Start CSR generation today. Submit to Google Trust Services or Sectigo. 2-5 day turnaround. Install in Cloudflare → SSL/TLS → Origin Server.

cisoPatchList.title: Confirm canonical domain with BPCL parent and resolve .com/.in ambiguity
cisoPatchList.fix: BPRL leadership decides canonical. Implement 301 redirect on non-canonical. Audit .in (ASP.NET) for security controls; if not authorised, file impersonation complaint.
cisoPatchList.validation: HTTP GET non-canonical returns 301 to canonical

cisoPatchList.title: WordPress core to 6.4.3+ (or current) for CVE-2024-31210
cisoPatchList.fix: WP CLI: wp core update. Disable plugin upload via wp-config: define('DISALLOW_FILE_MODS', true);
cisoPatchList.validation: wp core version returns ≥ 6.4.3; plugin-upload UI absent

cisoPatchList.title: Cloudflare origin protection + 'Always Use HTTPS'
cisoPatchList.fix: Cloudflare → SSL/TLS → Always Use HTTPS ON. Origin firewall: only Cloudflare IP ranges accepted.
cisoPatchList.validation: curl http:// redirects to https://; direct origin IP refused

cisoPatchList.title: Restrict admin-role accounts ≤ 2; enforce 2FA
cisoPatchList.fix: WP CLI: wp user list --role=administrator. Reduce to ≤ 2. Enforce 2FA via plugin (Wordfence / Two-Factor).

phase2.headlineQuestion: Which domain is the canonical BPRL site — bharatpetroresources.com or bharatpetroresources.in — and what is the cert-renewal status given 44 days to expiry?
phase2.coverageNotes: WordPress version not externally exposed; CVE-2024-31210 applicability conditional on version. .in domain audit not conducted (different stack). No admin-endpoint probing per carve-out. Origin IP not enumerated.
